purpose: Flag likely Standard Estonian inflectional leakage in intended VГөro text.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian active past participle -nud. VГөro expects -nU(q), practical -nu/-nГј, e.g. olnu/olnuq, saanu/saanuq.
rules.hint: Rewrite the verb as a VГөro past participle, not Estonian -nud.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian passive past participle -tud/-dud. VГөro uses passive past sg -t/-d and plural -tU(q)/-dU(q).
rules.hint: Check whether the VГөro form should end in -t/-d, -tu/-tГј, or -du/-dГј.

rules.message: Looks like Estonian past negation: ei + -nud/-tud. VГөro past negation normally uses es, e.g. es tiiq, or post-negation -Vs.
rules.hint: Use VГөro past negation, not Estonian ei teinud / ei olnud.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian 3rd singular present -b. VГөro 3sg present is normally endingless or -s-final, not -b.
rules.hint: Check whether this should be a VГөro 3sg form such as and, tulГө, kirotas, or elГӨs.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian 3rd plural present -vad. VГөro 3pl present is -vAq or -sEq, practical -vaq/-vГӨq or -sГөq/-seq.
rules.hint: Use a VГөro 3pl ending such as -vaq/-vГӨq or -sГөq/-seq.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian impersonal present -takse/-dakse. VГөro impersonal present is normally -tAs/-dAs.
rules.hint: Rewrite as a VГөro passive/impersonal form.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian simple past personal ending -sin/-sime/-site.
rules.hint: Use the VГөro simple past paradigm instead.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian conditional person ending -ksin/-ksime/-ksite. VГөro conditional is normally -si/-ssi or nU-type.
Looks like Standard Estonian illative -sse. VГөro standard illative uses -he, -tE, -dE, -Гҳ and other type-dependent forms.
Looks like Standard Estonian plural illative -desse/-tesse.
rules.hint: Use VГөro plural illative, often with -he or type-specific plural stem.

rules.message: Looks like Standard Estonian plural partitive or past-person ending -sid. VГөro plural partitive normally uses -sit/-it/-id, vowel plural, or isi/idsi-type forms.
rules.hint: Check whether this is an Estonian plural or past form.

rules.message: Looks like an Estonian da-/ta-infinitive without final q. VГөro infinitives normally keep q in practical spelling: -daq/-taq/-aq/-q.
rules.hint: If this is an infinitive, add/restore the VГөro infinitive form.

rules.message: Looks like Estonian mas-form. VГөro supine inessive is -mAn, e.g. -man/-mГӨn.
rules.hint: Check whether this should be VГөro -man/-mГӨn.

rules.message: Looks like Estonian maks-form. VГөro does not use Estonian -maks as the ordinary corresponding form.
rules.hint: Check whether the construction should be rewritten in VГөro.

rules.message: Looks like Estonian mata-form. VГөro supine abessive is -mAldA, e.g. -malda/-mГӨldГӨ.
rules.hint: Check whether this should be VГөro -malda/-mГӨldГӨ.

rules.message: Looks like Estonian des-/tes-gerund. VГөro gerundive uses endings like -dEn/-tEn/-n, e.g. kГөnГөldГөn, visatГөn.
rules.hint: Check whether this should be a VГөro gerundive.

rules.message: May be Estonian nominative plural -d. VГөro nominative plural is normally -q.
rules.hint: Only flag strongly if the context clearly needs nominative plural.

rules.message: May be Estonian translative -ks. VГөro translative is normally -s.
rules.hint: Only flag if this is clearly a noun/adjective case form.

rules.message: May be Estonian essive -na. Traditional VГөro does not productively use Estonian-style -nA essive.
rules.hint: Use only as a soft reminder; many ordinary words can end in -na.

do_not_flag_suffixes.reason: Estonian comitative -ga overlaps with current practical VГөro comitative -ga.
Estonian terminative -ni overlaps with practical VГөro terminative -ni.
VГөro supine elative can be -mAst, e.g. -mast/-mГӨst.
VГөro has present passive participle -tAv/-dAv, overlapping with Estonian.
